column1: Care Coordination and Support Personnel (CCaSP) - Shared Characteristics
(Shared attributes and functional patterns across roles)

Shared characteristics are the common attributes and patterns of participants that distinguish Care Coordination and Support Personnel roles independent of specific title, credentials, organizations, or care setting.

column3: The role is performed by an individual (not a system, service, or organization).
Primary purpose is enabling access, coordination, engagement, or continuity rather than direct clinical diagnosis/treatment.
Operates across healthcare, behavioral health, social care, community, and enabling services.
Identifies needs and connects individuals to appropriate services or resources.
Works with a shared care plan or equivalent plan of care to document, coordinate, guide, monitor, or support services and goals across settings and organizations.
Addresses logistical, administrative, social, financial, geographic, or knowledge barriers.
May interact over time rather than a single encounter.
Receives, facilitates, initiates, tracks, or closes referrals.
Supports transitions and continuity across settings and organizations.
Activities are organized around individual goals and preferences.
Frequently includes tracking completion, outcomes, or service engagement.
